Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class FlowStageStep

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowBaseElement
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowElement
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowStageStep
public class FlowStageStep extends FlowElement

Java class for FlowStageStep complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="FlowStageStep">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowElement">
       <sequence>
         <element name="actionName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="actionType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}InvocableActionType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="assignees" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowStageStepAssignee" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="entryActionInputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowStageStepEntryActionInputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="entryActionName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="entryActionOutputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowStageStepEntryActionOutputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="entryActionType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}InvocableActionType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="entryConditionLogic" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="entryConditions" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowCondition" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitActionInputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowStageStepExitActionInputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitActionName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitActionOutputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowStageStepExitActionOutputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitActionType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}InvocableActionType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="inputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowStageStepInputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="label" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="outputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowStageStepOutputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="requiresAsyncProcessing" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="stepSubtype" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowElementSubtype" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getAssignees

      public List<FlowStageStepAssignee> getAssignees()
      Gets the value of the assignees property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the assignees property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getAssignees().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowStageStepAssignee

      Returns:
      The value of the assignees property.

    • getEntryActionInputParameters

      public List<FlowStageStepEntryActionInputParameter> getEntryActionInputParameters()
      Gets the value of the entryActionInputParameters property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the entryActionInputParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getEntryActionInputParameters().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowStageStepEntryActionInputParameter

      Returns:
      The value of the entryActionInputParameters property.
